content: The human linguistic concept of reflexion as both 'reflecting on learning' (from high school language education) and physical light reflection can be mapped to LLM self-reflection as a dual-layered mechanism: just as reflexion means both bouncing light off a surface to see an image and deliberate self-review after learning, LLM self-reflection can be framed as simultaneously 'bouncing' off inconsistent problem-solving perspectives to expose flawed outputs and refining future reasoning from past errors. This dual framing suggests LLM self-reflection needs both the physical-like reflection step of surfacing inconsistencies and the deliberate learning-like reflection step of updating internal knowledge.
